# Flowbench

[![MIT License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE)
[![Node.js](https://img.shields.io/badge/Node.js-18%2B-green.svg)](https://nodejs.org/)

**Flowbench** is a lightweight, JSON-driven workflow engine for orchestrating LLMs, file operations, APIs, and more. Define processing graphs in plain JSON, run them via CLI for scripts/cron jobs, or use the local web UI with auto-generated forms for non-technical users.

**This entire README was self-generated by Flowbench** (via its own "Flowbench Help" graph) as of March 29, 2026.

## Key Features

- JSON Graph Definition with `$nodeId` references, caching for cycles/shared nodes, and runtime validation
- Dual Mode: CLI + web UI that auto-generates Bulma forms and output cards
- Extensible node system (drop a new `.js` file in `/nodes/` and it auto-registers)
- Local-first and secure (API keys stay in `.env`; graphs can be shared safely)
- Built-in support for Grok tools, image/TTS generation, Git, RSS, file blobs, and more

**Compared to alternatives**  
Lighter than n8n or Langflow (no visual bloat), more structured than raw LLM chaining scripts, and far more approachable for non-devs than most GitHub workflow repos.

**WIP**: Project is actively developed nightly. Editor is currently Monaco-based; a form-only graph editor is planned for April.

## Quickstart

1. Clone & install
   ```bash
   git clone https://github.com/johnmichaeljimenez/flowbench.git
   cd flowbench
   npm install
   ```

2. Add API keys (`.env` is gitignored)
   ```env
   API_KEY_GROK=your_xai_key
   BASE_URL_GROK=https://api.x.ai/v1
   # Optional: API_KEY_GEMINI, ELEVENLABS_API_KEY
   ```

3. Web UI (recommended)
   ```bash
   npm run dev
   ```
   Open http://localhost:3000, click "Load Graph", pick an example, fill the form, and run.

4. CLI
   ```bash
   npx flowbench graphs/chatbot/index.json --userInput.string "Tell me about Flowbench"
   ```

## Example Graphs

All graphs in `/graphs/` are standalone and ready to run.

| Graph                  | Description                                      | Highlights |
|------------------------|--------------------------------------------------|----------|
| `chatbot`              | Stateful chat with persistent memory & history   | Tools + file I/O |
| `flowbench help`       | Self-Q&A against the entire codebase             | Self-referential |
| `game idea generator`  | Raw notes → practical solo-dev game ideas        | Ensemble LLMs |
| `PH news`              | RSS → clean Philippines-focused summary          | No fluff |
| `project manager`      | Analyzes project folder + TODO + description     | Self-analysis |
| `quick assistant`      | Fast Q&A (Gemini Flash)                          | Lightweight |
| `resume generator`     | Scrap notes → ATS resume + market research       | Dual outputs |

## Extending

Add new nodes by creating `/nodes/myNode.js` (with `nodeMetadata`). New graph folders appear automatically in the UI.
